item.title: BB RESERVE HEIST: CID probe report submission deferred for 96th time
item.description: A Dhaka magistrate court on Thursday deferred the submission of the investigation report in the case over the Bangladesh Bank reserve heist until August 9, marking the 96th postponement since the case was filed a decade ago....

item.title: Govt discontinues austerity measures
item.description: The government has not yet extended its austerity measures in the current 2026-27 fiscal year, a move that would lift restrictions on foreign travel funded by the public exchequer and interest-free car loans for bureaucrats....

item.title: Cape Verde plot upset against Argentina
item.description: By any measure, Cape Verde should have next ​to no chance of knocking Argentina and Lionel Messi out of the World Cup on Friday, but ‌the Africans have huge confidence in their ability to defy the odds and keep their fairytale debut campaign alive....InternationalSports Competitions

item.title: Success of Banalata Express
item.description: BANGLADESH’S film industry has been going through a critical phase since the turn of the millennium, and there are hardly any signs of its recovery. The number of films, as well as the film theatres, has declined drastically since the early 2000s. The quantity of......

item.title: Temple born of violence, shaken by scandal
item.description: FEW religious sites in contemporary India carry the political, cultural and emotional weight of the Ram Mandir in Ayodhya. It stands on the ruins of the historic Babri Masjid, demolished in 1992 by Hindu nationalist groups, including affiliates of the......

item.title: Drivers of climate-smart farming
item.description: IN COASTAL Bangladesh, climate change is no longer an abstract forecast; it is visible in the white crust left by salt on farmland, the delayed sowing after waterlogging and the crop lost when an untimely storm arrives before harvest. Farmers know the risks. Yet awareness......

item.title: Bay of Bengal, Bangladesh, geo-strategy
item.description: THE Bay of Bengal is rapidly emerging as one of the world’s most strategically significant maritime regions. Beyond serving as a crucial route for international trade and energy transportation, it has become an arena of growing geopolitical competition and......Demographics

item.title: Govt must end teacher crisis in CHT schools
item.description: AN ACUTE shortage of teachers in government high schools in the hill districts of Bandarban, Khagrachari and Rangamati, collectively called the Chittagong Hill Tracts, where 43 per cent of the population is, as the 2022 census shows, from national minorities, is gravely......

item.title: In need of proactive dengue control steps, not reactive
item.description: THE re-emergence and dominance of a severe serotype of the dengue virus that health experts associate with causing neurological complications serve as a warning to both policy-makers and the public. The serotype, DENV-3, has, according to the......

item.title: Trump defends $1b crypto earnings
item.description: US president Donald Trump on Wednesday defended earning $1.2 billion from his family’s cryptocurrency activities last year, saying ‘everybody’s profiting’ from his time in power....

item.title: India, Japan vow closer work on critical minerals
item.description: India and Japan will work more closely on critical minerals to boost ‘resilience’ in their supply chains, prime minister Narendra Modi said Thursday, as the two huge Asian economies look to cut dependence on top exporter China....GeographicReference

item.title: AKIJ Cement holds annual business confnce
item.description: AKIJ Cement, a concern of industrial conglomerate AKIJ Resource, has recently held its annual business conference at a hotel in Bangkok, Thailand, said a press release....

item.title: NRBC Bank concludes dev training for entrepreneurs
item.description: NRBC Bank PLC on Thursday concluded a month-long entrepreneurship development training programme organised under initiative of the Bangladesh Bank, said a press release....

item.title: Mercantile Bank, Bangladesh Bank sign deal
item.description: Mercantile Bank PLC has recently signed an agreement with the Bangladesh Bank to provide low-interest loans to entrepreneurs under the cluster, said a press release....

item.title: I was just missing a goal: Yamal
item.description:
Spain star Lamine Yamal said Thursday he was delighted with his team's performance in their 3-0 win in the World Cup last 32 against Austria -- but admitted he would have liked to score.
...

item.title: France's Barcola expecting physical Paraguay clash
item.description: France forward Bradley Barcola warned on Thursday that Paraguay would bring a physical approach when the sides meet in the World Cup last 16 in Philadelphia....

item.title: Klopp poised to replace Nagelsmann as Germany coach: reports
item.description:
Julian Nagelsmann is reportedly set to be sacked as Germany coach, tabloid Bild reported Thursday, with former Liverpool manager Jurgen Klopp the favourite to take over in the dugout.
...

item.title: 'Zico' -- from Brazil to Egypt, a name steeped in World Cup lore
item.description: Brazilian icon Zico lit up three World Cups as a player -- and 40 years after his final appearance at football's extravaganza his Egyptian namesake is keeping the name in the spotlight....

item.title: Ghana have 'duty to Africa' to progress at World Cup
item.description: Ghana boss Carlos Queiroz said Thursday it was his team's ‘duty’ to ensure an African side reached the World Cup round of 16 after a string of knockout defeats for teams from the continent....InternationalSports Competitions

item.title: Faster than Mbappe: Australia flyer Bos races into World Cup conversation
item.description: Kylian Mbappe and Erling Haaland are two of the quickest players around -- but when it comes to pure speed they have a rival at the World Cup in Australian flyer Jordan Bos. ...

item.title: Egypt coach 'not sure' if Salah will start against Socceroos
item.description:
Egypt coach Hossam Hassan cast doubt on whether talisman Mohamed Salah will start their World Cup last-32 showdown with Australia on Friday in Texas following a hamstring problem.
...

item.title: Pochettino '200% Argentine' but embraces Americana
item.description: Mauricio Pochettino may be ‘200 per cent Argentine’ but the US coach has thrown himself into American culture, joyfully belting out ‘Take Me Home, Country Roads’ as his team celebrated their World Cup win over Bosnia....

item.title: Scaloni warns Argentina that Cape Verde success 'no accident'
item.description:
Cape Verde's stunning progress to the World Cup knockout stages is ‘no accident’ said Argentina boss Lionel Scaloni as he warned his side about the threat posed by the Blue Sharks in their last 32 tie.
...

item.title: Brazil's Paqueta ruled out of clash with Norway
item.description: Brazil midfielder Lucas Paqueta will miss Sunday's World Cup last-16 game against Norway with a hamstring injury, a source at the Brazilian Football Confederation told AFP on Thursday....GeographicReference

item.title: Spain ease past Austria to round of 16
item.description: Spain coasted past Austria and into the World Cup last-16 on Thursday, thoroughly outclassing their opponents in a 3-0 knockout win with a brace from Mikel Oyarzabal and a Pedro Porro header....

item.title: Al Mujahidi remained uncompromising in his principles: discussion
item.description: Speakers at a programme in Dhaka on Thursday said that poet Al Mujahidi remained uncompromising in his literary works and principles....GeographicReference

item.title: IUB, Norwegian embassy sign agreement for Ibsen-Fosse fest
item.description: The Independent University, Bangladesh and the Royal Norwegian embassy in Dhaka have recently signed an agreement to support the upcoming Henrik Ibsen and Jon Fosse Festival in Bangladesh....

item.title: DU ahead in ranking but lags in research: pro-VC Salam
item.description: Dhaka University pro-vice chancellor (academic) professor Abdus Salam said on Thursday that it is ahead in various rankings but lagging in its research environment, which needs to be developed....

item.title: One killed in nine shrine attacks in six months: report
item.description: At least nine shrines were targeted in a series of attacks during the first half of the year, leaving one person dead and ten others injured, according to a report published by Maqam: Center for Sufi Heritage....

item.title: SEU holds orientation for new students
item.description: The Southeast University has welcomed newly admitted students for the summer 2026 semester with an orientation programme on its campus at Tejgaon in Dhaka recently....Demographics

item.title: DU releases integrated tree census results
item.description: The University of Dhaka released the results of its first-ever integrated tree census 2025 at the Nabab Nawab Ali Chowdhury Senate Bhaban of DU on Thursday....

item.title: Govt to launch OSS, pilot projects for persons with disabilities: Farzana
item.description: State minister for social welfare Farzana Sharmin at a meeting in Dhaka on Thursday said that the government was working through an inter-ministerial framework to bring all rightful facilities for persons with disabilities under a single one-stop service umbrella....

item.title: ULAB celebrates CPC’s founding anniversary
item.description: The University of Liberal Arts Bangladesh hosted a cultural celebration, marking the 105th anniversary of the founding of the Communist Party of China and the 90th anniversary of the victory of the Long March, on its campus in Dhaka on Wednesday....

item.title: No real difference between constitution amendment and reform: chief whip
item.description: Chief whip in the Jatiya Sangsad Md Nurul Islam said on Thursday that he saw no fundamental difference between the government’s proposal to amend the constitution and the opposition’s call for constitutional reform....

item.title: Gono Bishwabidyalay holds farewell for Prof Mansur Musa
item.description: The Gono Bishwabidyalay held a farewell ceremony for linguist, educationist and researcher Professor Mansur Musa at the university at Savar in Dhaka on Thursday....

item.title: Minister asks JICA for more transport aid
item.description: Road transport and bridges, railways and shipping minister Shaikh Rabiul Alam on Thursday said that Bangladesh was committed to building a safe, modern, sustainable and integrated transport system under the leadership of prime minister Tarique Rahman....

item.title: IU forms probe body over sexual harassment
item.description: The Islamic University authorities have formed a three-member probe committee to investigate the alleged sexual harassment of a first-year female student by an unidentified man on the campus in Kushtia....

item.title: HSTU expels 6 for life over campus violence
item.description: Hajee Mohammad DaneshScienceand Technology University has expelled six students for life and penalised 60 others over a clash between the students of two residential halls and an attack on an assistant proctor....

item.title: Search body invites CVs for ACC chief, commissioner positions
item.description: The search committee, formed to reconstitute the Anti-Corruption Commission (ACC), has invited applications or CVs from eligible and interested candidates for the posts of the ACC chairman and commissioners....MultilateralOrganizations

item.title: Draft law to curtail NHRC independence: TIB
item.description: Transparency International Bangladesh on Thursday expressed concern over the proposed draft of the National Human Rights Commission Act, saying that if enacted in its current form, it would significantly weaken the commission’s independence and authority....

item.title: Ex-AL MP Ashika Sultana sent to jail
item.description: A metropolitan magistrate court in Dhaka on Thursday sent former Awami League lawmaker Ashika Sultana to jail after the completion of her remand in two phases in a case filed under the Anti-Terrorism Act with Mirpur police station....

item.title: Hormuz is not the playground of America: Iran
item.description: All commercial vessels are obliged to take the Iranian-designated maritime route for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz or face the consequences, Iran’s military headquarters warns according to Al Jazeera on Thursday....

item.title: Jamaat leader returns bicycle meant for poor student amid backlash
item.description: A local Jamaat leader in Kotchandpur upazila of Jhenaidah has come under the spotlight after taking a government-allocated bicycle intended for an underprivileged madrassa student and gifting it to his granddaughter....Demographics

item.title: 36pc students skip HSC, equivalent exams: minister
item.description: As many as 543,989 students, or 36.46 per cent of the 1,491,932 regular students registered for the Higher Secondary Certificate (HSC) and equivalent examinations, did not fill out the forms required to sit for the exams, said education minister......

item.title: British-era railway law reform drags on for seven years
item.description: Moves for amending the 136-year old British-era railways law by the successive governments to improve passengers’ services and security and check fraudulences have dragged on for last seven years without any progress. ...

item.title: Govt aims to boost healthcare with advanced medical equipment: minister
item.description: Health and family welfare minister Sardar Md Sakhawat Husain on Thursday said that the government was committed to upgrading the quality of healthcare across the country by equipping hospitals with state-of-the-art medical technology....

item.title: US envoy hails military, people-to-people ties with Bangladesh
item.description: US ambassador to Bangladesh Brent T Christensen on Thursday said military-to-military cooperation and strong people-to-people ties remain the foundation of the Bangladesh-US partnership, describing music as a unique bridge that brings the two nations closer....GeographicReference

item.title: PM opens Nazrul Year programmes
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Thursday said National Poet Kazi Nazrul Islam’s pen was a sharp weapon against colonial rule, oppression, exploitation, inequality, discrimination, superstition and every form of injustice and ugliness....

item.title: Russia-Ukraine war toll tops two million
item.description: Russia’s invasion of Ukraine has caused more than two million military casualties, with Moscow’s forces bearing the brunt of the losses, according to a study published Wednesday by a US think tank....

item.title: Whereabouts of Suu Kyi unknown in Myanmar
item.description: Where is Aung San Suu Kyi? Myanmar’s deposed leader is under house arrest in the capital Naypyidaw, according to her captors, but exactly where is hard to say in a city experts describe as purpose-built to guard its closed-ranks rulers’ secrets....
